title: overlayfs: fs does not support file handles, falling back to xino=off on ubuntu22
body:
### Issue Description

After installing podman 3.4.4 on ubuntu22 we continuously see these messages in dmesg or /var/log/messages . I have tried several configurations but the errors dont go away

[Thu Aug 29 08:15:02 2024] overlayfs: fs on '/home/<username>/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/opaque-bug-check115720875/l2' does not support file handles, falling back to xino=off.
[Thu Aug 29 08:37:34 2024] overlayfs: fs on '/home/<username>/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/compat3416904402/lower1' does not support file handles, falling back to xino=off.
[Thu Aug 29 08:37:35 2024] overlayfs: fs on '/home/<username>/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/metacopy-check3075981219/l1' does not support file handles, falling back to xino=off.
[Thu Aug 29 08:37:35 2024] overlayfs: fs on '/home/<username>/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/opaque-bug-check974546424/l2' does not support file handles, falling back to xino=off.


~# mount | grep /home
/dev/mapper/vg00-home on /home type ext4 (rw,nosuid,nodev,relatime)

# cat /etc/containers/storage.conf
[storage]
driver = "overlay2"
[storage.options.overlay]
mountopt = "xino=off"
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"

~# podman --version
podman version 3.4.4

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Have an ubuntu22 machine
2. install podman

### Describe the results you received

After installing podman 3.4.4 on ubuntu22 we continuously see these messages in dmesg or /var/log/messages . I have tried several configurations but the errors dont go away


### Describe the results you expected

No errors related to xino=off

### Additional information

comments.body: 3.4.4 is ancient and we only support the latest version upstream

> 3.4.4 is ancient and we only support the latest version upstream

That is default version on ubuntu22

The same issue happens with podman 5.2.2

**dmesg log**
```
[  +0.000007] overlayfs: fs on '/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/compat1849726343/lower1' does not support file handles, falling back to xino=off.
[  +0.000003] overlayfs: fs on '/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/metacopy-check318920475/l1' does not support file handles, falling back to xino=off.
[  +0.000002] overlayfs: fs on '/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/opaque-bug-check1730570080/l2' does not support file handles, falling back to xino=off.
[  +0.000004] overlayfs: fs on '/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/volatile-check1621200136/lower' does not support file handles, falling back to xino=off.
[  +0.000002] overlayfs: fs on '/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l/I5S7L5V3COSOJSO7CF3MYFBDXB' does not support file handles, falling back to xino=off.
```
**/etc/containers/storage.conf**
```
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/run/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
[storage.options]
additionalimagestores = [
]
pull_options = {enable_partial_images = "true", use_hard_links = "false", ostree_repos=""}
[storage.options.overlay]
mountopt = "nodev"

```

xino doesn't seem to be a mount option set anywhere in our code base which means it likely just uses the kernel default and as your lower file systems doesn't seem to support file handles you always get this warning.

Reading the kernel docs https://docs.kernel.org/filesystems/overlayfs.html#xino seems to indicate that is can be set with CONFIG_OVERLAY_FS_XINO_AUTO kernel option so I assume this is turned on on your kernel so this doesn't sounds like a podman problem to me.

Can you try this config
```
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
runroot = "/run/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
[storage.options]
additionalimagestores = [
]
pull_options = {enable_partial_images = "true", use_hard_links = "false", ostree_repos=""}
[storage.options.overlay]
mountopt = "nodev,xino=off"
```

and strace the mount syscalls (`strace -f -e mount ...`) to see what options are being passed

Hello 
> Reading the kernel docs https://docs.kernel.org/filesystems/overlayfs.html#xino seems to indicate that is can be set with CONFIG_OVERLAY_FS_XINO_AUTO kernel option so I assume this is turned on on your kernel so this 
doesn't sounds like a podman problem to me.
This  kernel option is definitely on in default archlunux kernel.
```
zgrep CONFIG_OVERLAY_FS_XINO_AUTO /proc/config.gz                                                                                                                                                    
CONFIG_OVERLAY_FS_XINO_AUTO=y
```



                                        

> Can you try this config
> 
> ```
> [storage]
> driver = "overlay"
> runroot = "/run/containers/storage"
> graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"
> [storage.options]
> additionalimagestores = [
> ]
> pull_options = {enable_partial_images = "true", use_hard_links = "false", ostree_repos=""}
> [storage.options.overlay]
> mountopt = "nodev,xino=off"
> ```
> 
> and strace the mount syscalls (`strace -f -e mount ...`) to see what options are being passed

I've tried running podman with `mountopt = "nodev,xino=off"` the error still occurs in the logs. 

As I'm new to this can you point me to the right direction of what exactly should I `strace -f -e mount` 

I'm evaluating podman using quadlet files on Archlinux with ext4 as the underlying fs if it helps, I also
tried that the same quadlet on Rocky 9.4 (podman 4.9.4-rhel,ext4) and it does not produce errors on startup except this one:
`overlayfs: idmapped layers are currently not supported`

> As I'm new to this can you point me to the right direction of what exactly should I strace -f -e mount

Whatever podman command triggering the warnings, i.e. 
strace -f -e mount podman run ...

I've attached the [podman.strace.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16846387/podman.strace.log) , the command produced the same error 

```

Sep 03 12:23:20 server kernel: overlayfs: upper fs does not support file handles, falling back to index=off.
Sep 03 12:23:20 server kernel: overlayfs: fs on '/home/user/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l/5KBVGOMUKOLZQS6D6NWM23URLO' does not support file handles, falling back to xino=off.```

ah strace truncated the options as they are to long you need to add `-s 4096`

Anyhow I see the problem with the config file, the root storage.conf file is not used for the rootless users except the rootless_storage_path option. The config file must be created in `~/.config/containers/storage.conf` as rootless in order for the mount options to take effect. Something like this seems to apply the storage.conf options:
```
[storage]
driver = "overlay"
[storage.options]
[storage.options.overlay]
mountopt = "nodev,metacopy=on,xino=off"
```


But regardless this seems to be something wrong with your kernel mount options I think, we do not set xino=on or xino=auto so the fact that it defaults to that and it fails on your fs is odd and this has nothing to so with podman.

If you run something like this (on the same fs as the podman storage) I assume it generates the same warning
```
$ mkdir lower work upper merged
$ unshare -rm mount -t overlay overlay -o lowerdir=lower,upperdir=upper,workdir=work merged
```

The config i tried on user level and it didnt help and yes it generates same warning when i run the commands you shared

[Tue Sep  3 09:09:22 2024] overlayfs: fs on 'lower' does not support file handles, falling back to xino=off.
Ack sounds like some problem with the kernel config and/or filesystem then. I cannot help you with that.
